children.heading: Entertainment, amusement, recreation, or qualified transportation fringes
children.label: a Entertainment, amusement, recreation, or qualified transportation fringes

children.heading: Activity
children.label: A Activity
children.text: With respect to an activity which is of a type generally considered to constitute entertainment, amusement, or recreation, or

children.heading: Facility
children.label: B Facility
children.text: With respect to a facility used in connection with an activity referred to in subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: Dues or fees to any social, athletic, or sporting club or organization shall be treated as items with respect to facilities.

children.label: B
children.text: An activity described in section 212 shall be treated as a trade or business.

children.heading: Denial of deduction for club dues
children.label: 3 Denial of deduction for club dues
children.text: Notwithstanding the preceding provisions of this subsection, no deduction shall be allowed under this chapter for amounts paid or incurred for membership in any club organized for business, pleasure, recreation, or other social purpose.

children.heading: Qualified transportation fringes
children.label: 4 Qualified transportation fringes
children.text: No deduction shall be allowed under this chapter for the expense of any qualified transportation fringe (as defined in section 132(f)) provided to an employee of the taxpayer.

children.text: an item having a cost to the taxpayer not in excess of $4.00 on which the name of the taxpayer is clearly and permanently imprinted and which is one of a number of identical items distributed generally by the taxpayer, or

children.label: B
children.text: a sign, display rack, or other promotional material to be used on the business premises of the recipient.

children.label: A
children.text: In the case of a gift by a partnership, the limitation contained in paragraph (1) shall apply to the partnership as well as to each member thereof.

children.label: B
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), a husband and wife shall be treated as one taxpayer.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of any individual who travels outside the United States away from home in pursuit of a trade or business or in pursuit of an activity described in section 212, no deduction shall be allowed under section 162 or section 212 for that portion of the expenses of such travel otherwise allowable under such section which, under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, is not allocable to such trade or business or to such activity.

children.label: A
children.text: such travel does not exceed one week, or

children.label: B
children.text: the portion of the time of travel outside the United States away from home which is not attributable to the pursuit of the taxpayer’s trade or business or an activity described in section 212 is less than 25 percent of the total time on such travel.

children.heading: Domestic travel excluded
children.label: 3 Domestic travel excluded
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, travel outside the United States does not include any travel from one point in the United States to another point in the United States.

children.heading: Specific exceptions to application of subsection (a)
children.label: e Specific exceptions to application of subsection (a)

children.heading: Food and beverages for employees
children.label: 1 Food and beverages for employees
children.text: Expenses for food and beverages (and facilities used in connection therewith) furnished on the business premises of the taxpayer primarily for his employees.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), expenses for goods, services, and facilities, to the extent that the expenses are treated by the taxpayer, with respect to the recipient of the entertainment, amusement, or recreation, as compensation to an employee on the taxpayer’s return of tax under this chapter and as wages to such employee for purposes of chapter 24 (relating to withholding of income tax at source on wages).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of a recipient who is a specified individual, subparagraph (A) and paragraph (9) shall each be applied by substituting “to the extent that the expenses do not exceed the amount of the expenses which” for “to the extent that the expenses”.

children.label: I
children.text: is subject to the requirements of section 16(a) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 with respect to the taxpayer or a related party to the taxpayer, or

children.label: II
children.text: would be subject to such requirements if the taxpayer (or such related party) were an issuer of equity securities referred to in such section.

children.label: A
children.text: where the services are performed for an employer, only if the employer has not treated such expenses in the manner provided in paragraph (2), or

children.label: B
children.text: where the services are performed for a person other than an employer, only if the taxpayer accounts (to the extent provided by subsection (d)) to such person.

children.heading: Recreational, etc., expenses for employees
children.label: 4 Recreational, etc., expenses for employees
children.text: Expenses for recreational, social, or similar activities (including facilities therefor) primarily for the benefit of employees (other than employees who are highly compensated employees (within the meaning of section 414(q))). For purposes of this paragraph, an individual owning less than a 10-percent interest in the taxpayer’s trade or business shall not be considered a shareholder or other owner, and for such purposes an individual shall be treated as owning any interest owned by a member of his family (within the meaning of section 267(c)(4)). This paragraph shall not apply for purposes of subsection (a)(3).

children.heading: Employees, stockholder, etc., business meetings
children.label: 5 Employees, stockholder, etc., business meetings
children.text: Expenses incurred by a taxpayer which are directly related to business meetings of his employees, stockholders, agents, or directors.

children.heading: Meetings of business leagues, etc.
children.label: 6 Meetings of business leagues, etc.
children.text: Expenses directly related and necessary to attendance at a business meeting or convention of any organization described in section 501(c)(6) (relating to business leagues, chambers of commerce, real estate boards, and boards of trade) and exempt from taxation under section 501(a).

children.heading: Items available to public
children.label: 7 Items available to public
children.text: Expenses for goods, services, and facilities made available by the taxpayer to the general public.

children.heading: Entertainment sold to customers
children.label: 8 Entertainment sold to customers
children.text: Expenses for goods or services (including the use of facilities) which are sold by the taxpayer in a bona fide transaction for an adequate and full consideration in money or money’s worth.

children.heading: Expenses includible in income of persons who are not employees
children.label: 9 Expenses includible in income of persons who are not employees
children.text: Expenses paid or incurred by the taxpayer for goods, services, and facilities to the extent that the expenses are includible in the gross income of a recipient of the entertainment, amusement, or recreation who is not an employee of the taxpayer as compensation for services rendered or as a prize or award under section 74. The preceding sentence shall not apply to any amount paid or incurred by the taxpayer if such amount is required to be included (or would be so required except that the amount is less than $600) in any information return filed by such taxpayer under part III of subchapter A of chapter 61 and is not so included.

children.heading: Interest, taxes, casualty losses, etc.
children.label: f Interest, taxes, casualty losses, etc.
children.text: This section shall not apply to any deduction allowable to the taxpayer without regard to its connection with his trade or business (or with his income-producing activity). In the case of a taxpayer which is not an individual, the preceding sentence shall be applied as if it were an individual.

children.heading: Treatment of entertainment, etc., type facility
children.label: g Treatment of entertainment, etc., type facility
children.text: For purposes of this chapter, if deductions are disallowed under subsection (a) with respect to any portion of a facility, such portion shall be treated as an asset which is used for personal, living, and family purposes (and not as an asset used in the trade or business).

children.label: B
children.text: the purposes and activities of the sponsoring organizations or groups,

children.label: C

children.label: D

children.text: the cruise ship is a vessel registered in the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: all ports of call of such cruise ship are located in the United States or in possessions of the United States.

children.heading: North American area
children.label: A North American area
children.text: The term “North American area” means the United States, its possessions, and the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands, and Canada and Mexico.

children.heading: Cruise ship
children.label: B Cruise ship
children.text: The term “cruise ship” means any vessel sailing within or without the territorial waters of the United States.

children.heading: Subsection to apply to employer as well as to traveler
children.label: 4 Subsection to apply to employer as well as to traveler
A
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), this subsection shall apply to deductions otherwise allowable under section 162 to any person, whether or not such person is the individual attending the convention, seminar, or similar meeting.

children.label: B
children.text: This subsection shall not deny a deduction to any person other than the individual attending the convention, seminar, or similar meeting with respect to any amount paid by such person to or on behalf of such individual if includible in the gross income of such individual. The preceding sentence shall not apply if the amount is required to be included in any information return filed by such person under part III of subchapter A of chapter 61 and is not so included.

children.label: iii

children.label: ii

children.label: iii
children.text: such other information as may be required in regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Treatment of conventions in certain Caribbean countries
children.label: 6 Treatment of conventions in certain Caribbean countries
i
children.text: there is in effect a bilateral or multilateral agreement described in subparagraph (C) between such country and the United States providing for the exchange of information between the United States and such country, and

children.label: ii
children.text: there is not in effect a finding by the Secretary that the tax laws of such country discriminate against conventions held in the United States.

children.heading: Beneficiary country
children.label: B Beneficiary country
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “beneficiary country” has the meaning given to such term by section 212(a)(1)(A) of the Caribbean Basin Economic Recovery Act; except that such term shall include Bermuda.

children.heading: Authority to conclude exchange of information agreements
children.label: C Authority to conclude exchange of information agreements

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to negotiate and conclude an agreement for the exchange of information with any beneficiary country. Except as provided in clause (ii), an exchange of information agreement shall provide for the exchange of such information (not limited to information concerning nationals or residents of the United States or the beneficiary country) as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out and enforce the tax laws of the United States and the beneficiary country (whether criminal or civil proceedings), including information which may otherwise be subject to nondisclosure provisions of the local law of the beneficiary country such as provisions respecting bank secrecy and bearer shares. The exchange of information agreement shall be terminable by either country on reasonable notice and shall provide that information received by either country will be disclosed only to persons or authorities (including courts and administrative bodies) involved in the administration or oversight of, or in the determination of appeals in respect of, taxes of the United States or the beneficiary country and will be used by such persons or authorities only for such purposes.

children.heading: Nondisclosure of qualified confidential information sought for civil tax purposes
children.label: ii Nondisclosure of qualified confidential information sought for civil tax purposes
I
children.text: the Secretary of the Treasury, after making all reasonable efforts to negotiate an agreement which includes the exchange of such information, determines that such an agreement cannot be negotiated but that the agreement which was negotiated will significantly assist in the administration and enforcement of the tax laws of the United States, and

children.label: II
children.text: the President determines that the agreement as negotiated is in the national security interest of the United States.

children.heading: Qualified confidential information defined
children.label: iii Qualified confidential information defined
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “qualified confidential information” means information which is subject to the nondisclosure provisions of any local law of the beneficiary country regarding bank secrecy or ownership of bearer shares.

children.heading: Civil tax purposes
children.label: iv Civil tax purposes
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the determination of whether information is sought only for civil tax purposes shall be made by the requesting party.

children.heading: Coordination with other provisions
children.label: D Coordination with other provisions
children.text: Any exchange of information agreement negotiated under subparagraph (C) shall be treated as an income tax convention for purposes of section 6103(k)(4). The Secretary may exercise his authority under subchapter A of chapter 78 to carry out any obligation of the United States under an agreement referred to in subparagraph (C).

children.heading: Determinations published in the Federal Register
children.label: E Determinations published in the Federal Register
i
children.text: any determination by the President under subparagraph (C)(ii) (including the reasons for such determination),

children.label: ii
children.text: any determination by the Secretary under subparagraph (C)(ii) (including the reasons for such determination), and

children.label: iii
children.text: any finding by the Secretary under subparagraph (A)(ii) (and any termination thereof).

children.heading: Seminars, etc. for section 212 purposes
children.label: 7 Seminars, etc. for section 212 purposes
children.text: No deduction shall be allowed under section 212 for expenses allocable to a convention, seminar, or similar meeting.

children.heading: Qualified nonpersonal use vehicle
children.label: i Qualified nonpersonal use vehicle
children.text: For purposes of subsection (d), the term “qualified nonpersonal use vehicle” means any vehicle which, by reason of its nature, is not likely to be used more than a de minimis amount for personal purposes.

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: No deduction shall be allowed under section 162 or section 212 for the cost of an employee achievement award except to the extent that such cost does not exceed the deduction limitations of paragraph (2).

children.label: A

children.label: B
children.text: which is a qualified plan award, when added to the cost to the employer for all other employee achievement awards made to such employee during the taxable year (including employee achievement awards which are not qualified plan awards), shall not exceed $1,600.

children.label: II
children.text: awarded as part of a meaningful presentation, and

children.label: III
children.text: awarded under conditions and circumstances that do not create a significant likelihood of the payment of disguised compensation.

children.label: I
children.text: cash, cash equivalents, gift cards, gift coupons, or gift certificates (other than arrangements conferring only the right to select and receive tangible personal property from a limited array of such items pre-selected or pre-approved by the employer), or

children.label: II
children.text: vacations, meals, lodging, tickets to theater or sporting events, stocks, bonds, other securities, and other similar items.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “qualified plan award” means an employee achievement award awarded as part of an established written plan or program of the taxpayer which does not discriminate in favor of highly compensated employees (within the meaning of section 414(q)) as to eligibility or benefits.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: An employee achievement award shall not be treated as a qualified plan award for any taxable year if the average cost of all employee achievement awards which are provided by the employer during the year, and which would be qualified plan awards but for this subparagraph, exceeds $400. For purposes of the preceding sentence, average cost shall be determined by including the entire cost of qualified plan awards, without taking into account employee achievement awards of nominal value.

children.heading: Partnerships
children.label: A Partnerships
children.text: In the case of an employee achievement award made by a partnership, the deduction limitations contained in paragraph (2) shall apply to the partnership as well as to each member thereof.

children.heading: Length of service awards
children.label: B Length of service awards
children.text: An item shall not be treated as having been provided for length of service achievement if the item is received during the recipient’s 1st 5 years of employment or if the recipient received a length of service achievement award (other than an award excludable under section 132(e)(1)) during that year or any of the prior 4 years.

children.label: ii
children.text: such item is awarded to a manager, administrator, clerical employee, or other professional employee.

children.heading: Transportation and commuting benefits
children.label: l Transportation and commuting benefits
children.text: No deduction shall be allowed under this chapter for any expense incurred for providing any transportation, or any payment or reimbursement, to an employee of the taxpayer in connection with travel between the employee’s residence and place of employment, except as necessary for ensuring the safety of the employee.

children.heading: Additional limitations on travel expenses
children.label: m Additional limitations on travel expenses

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No deduction shall be allowed under this chapter for expenses incurred for transportation by water to the extent such expenses exceed twice the aggregate per diem amounts for days of such transportation. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the term “per diem amounts” means the highest amount generally allowable with respect to a day to employees of the executive branch of the Federal Government for per diem while away from home but serving in the United States.

children.heading: Travel as form of education
children.label: 2 Travel as form of education
children.text: No deduction shall be allowed under this chapter for expenses for travel as a form of education.

children.label: 3 Travel expenses of spouse, dependent, or others
A

children.label: B

children.label: C
children.text: such expenses would otherwise be deductible by the spouse, dependent, or other individual.

children.heading: Only 50 percent of meal expenses allowed as deduction
children.label: n Only 50 percent of meal expenses allowed as deduction

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The amount allowable as a deduction under this chapter for any expense for food or beverages shall not exceed 50 percent of the amount of such expense which would (but for this paragraph) be allowable as a deduction under this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: such expense is described in paragraph (2), (3), (4), (7), (8), or (9) of subsection (e),

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of an employer who pays or reimburses moving expenses of an employee, such expenses are includible in the income of the employee under section 82,

children.label: i
children.text: required by any Federal law to be provided to crew members of a commercial vessel,

children.label: I
children.text: which is operating on the Great Lakes, the Saint Lawrence Seaway, or any inland waterway of the United States, and

children.label: II
children.text: which is of a kind which would be required by Federal law to provide food and beverages to crew members if it were operated at sea,

children.label: iii
children.text: provided on an oil or gas platform or drilling rig if the platform or rig is located offshore,

children.label: iv
children.text: provided on an oil or gas platform or drilling rig, or at a support camp which is in proximity and integral to such platform or rig, if the platform or rig is located in the United States north of 54 degrees north latitude, or

children.label: I
children.text: on a fishing vessel, fish processing vessel, or fish tender vessel (as such terms are defined in , United States Code), orsection 2101 of title 46

children.label: aa
children.text: is located in the United States north of 50 degrees north latitude, and

children.label: bb
children.text: is not located in a metropolitan statistical area (within the meaning of section 143(k)(2)(B)), or

children.label: i
children.text: for food or beverages provided by a restaurant, and

children.label: ii
children.text: paid or incurred before .January 1, 2023

children.heading: Special rule for individuals subject to Federal hours of service
children.label: 3 Special rule for individuals subject to Federal hours of service
children.text: In the case of any expenses for food or beverages consumed while away from home (within the meaning of section 162(a)(2)) by an individual during, or incident to, the period of duty subject to the hours of service limitations of the Department of Transportation, paragraph (1) shall be applied by substituting “80 percent” for “50 percent”.

children.heading: Meals provided at convenience of employer
children.label: o Meals provided at convenience of employer

children.text: any expense for the operation of a facility described in section 132(e)(2), and any expense for food or beverages, including under section 132(e)(1), associated with such facility, or
any expense for meals described in section 119(a).

children.heading: Regulatory authority
children.label: p Regulatory authority
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as he may deem necessary to carry out the purposes of this section, including regulations prescribing whether subsection (a) or subsection (b) applies in cases where both such subsections would otherwise apply.
